mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate rising above a restrictive threshold of 2.5% increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity market performance over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a walk-forward test period covering one full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate reflects a strengthening labor market and robust economic activity. This supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns as a persistent, always-on signal.
failure_condition: The signal's rank information coefficient (IC) versus 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both housing starts and retail sales indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, negatively impacting corporate revenues and leading to lower equity prices.
candidate_trigger: 3-month % change in HOUST < 0 AND 3-month % change in RSAFS < 0
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in over 50% of instances where both series show a 3-month percentage decline.

mechanism_narrative: The momentum of industrial production serves as a direct proxy for the health of the economy's supply side. Positive momentum indicates robust output, translating to higher corporate profits and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and positive (p > 0.05) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the policy rate remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve', necessitating future aggressive tightening that will trigger an economic slowdown and negatively impact equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS < 3.0%
failure_condition: In periods matching the trigger condition, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in monthly nonfarm payrolls growth below a critical threshold signals an imminent deterioration in the labor market, a key indicator of recession, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not experience a drawdown of at least 5% within the 60 trading days following more than half of the triggered signals.

mechanism_narrative: The year-over-year growth rate of real GDP is a fundamental measure of economic momentum. Positive growth supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive short-term equity returns, even at high frequency.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between year-over-year GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically different from zero over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the consumer price index above a high threshold compresses corporate profit margins via input costs and forces monetary tightening, leading to lower equity valuations and negative returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months where year-over-year inflation exceeds the trigger threshold.
